summary: HRM's success on ARC-AGI-1 stems from its iterative refinement and task augmentation, not its hierarchical design. The model uses an outer loop where each iteration refines the prediction, halting based on a learned confidence score. This process, combined with augmenting training tasks to expose underlying rules, allows it to memorize solutions effectively. In fact, a standard transformer of similar size performs equally well when using the same refinement and augmentation techniques. The hierarchical architecture itself contributes little; the key is the refinement process and the use of augmentations to avoid overfitting specific task features.
